codeReview.summary: The fix still does not exercise the required real SwiftUI entrypoints or enforce strict same-anchor ordered parity, so the original cross-flow and Session Detail blockers remain unresolved.

issues.description: These selectors still exercise `SessionDetailContextPackRow` and local helper state rather than `SessionDetailView` presentation. `test_rowVisibility_requiresIndexingEnabledAndConversation`, `test_rowPlacementBelowSessionLogAction`, `test_tapPresentsContextPackSheet`, `test_anchorCarriesSelectedSessionIdentity`, `test_anchorCarriesSelectedProjectScope`, `test_reopenReanchorsToCurrentSession`, and `test_sessionDetailContextPackEntryReachableFromDashboardFlow` prove callback values or database lookups only; the only `SessionDetailView` instantiation is the nil-conversation smoke test. That leaves VAL-CTXDETAIL-001/002/003/004/005/007/009/010 unproven at the real SwiftUI entry surface.

`assembleAnchored`/`assembleUnanchored` still call `ContextPackService.assemble` directly, so the suite never drives `DashboardView`, `SessionDetailView`, or `ContextPackSheet`. `test_dashboardAndSessionDetailProduceEquivalentPackForSameAnchor` compares anchored vs unanchored packs only by inclusion/count/determinism, and never asserts `anchoredIds == unanchoredIds`; `test_sameAnchorSemanticParityAcrossEntrypoints` compares normalized concatenated bodies but still not ordered session-id parity. The original cross-entry regression is therefore still possible.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `context-pack-ui-worker` procedure would be easier to follow if it included one concrete Dashboard -> SessionDetail -> ContextPackSheet harness example and an explicit warning against treating row/service helpers as sufficient CTXDETAIL/CTXCROSS coverage.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: AgentLensTests/Active/ContextPackSessionDetailSurfaceTests.swift:161-421 and AgentLensTests/Active/ContextPackCrossFlowTests.swift:198-449 still rely on `SessionDetailContextPackRow`/`ContextPackService.assemble` rather than the end-to-end surfaces.

summary: The fix still does not exercise the required real SwiftUI entrypoints or enforce strict same-anchor ordered parity, so the original cross-flow and Session Detail blockers remain unresolved.
